source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Dropped Object - Sheared Bolts and Falling Actuator Plates
actions_taken: BHP and Transocean initiated review of equipment modifications
Plan to install bushing saver and guide to prevent future occurrences
event.summary: On January 18, 2015, during drilling operations at 2140 hours, the PS-1000 drill pipe slips set unexpectedly while the driller was rotating at 180 RPM after making a connection. The sudden stop of the drill string created immense torque that was transferred to the MDDM (Modular Derrick Drilling Machine), causing bolts on two of the I-BOP's actuator plates to be sheared. One plate fell approximately 30 feet to the bridge-racker walkway, and the other fell approximately 130 feet to the rig floor. Each plate weighed 19 pounds. No personnel were in the impact areas and no injuries occurred. The root cause was a combination of poor weather conditions that prevented rig repositioning and equipment failure where downward force from the drill string overcame the hydraulic pressure holding the slips open.
recommendations: Install bushing saver and guide to prevent future slips closure
Modify MHWirth/Aker I-BOP actuator plates and lever arms to prevent contact during drilling operations
key_phrases: PS-1000 drill pipe slips set unexpectedly
drill string rubbing against rotary and slips

hazards.name: High-pressure drill string rotation and movement
Adverse weather conditions affecting rig positioning
Mechanical equipment failure in pipe slips

threats.name: Drill string misalignment causing excessive downward force on slips
Hydraulic pressure insufficient to maintain slips in open position
Unexpected closure of pipe slips during rotation

consequences.name: Shearing of I-BOP actuator plate bolts
Dropped object - actuator plates falling from height
Potential personnel injury from falling objects
Loss of I-BOP safety valve functionality

controls.name: Hydraulic pressure system maintaining slips in open position
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent unintended slips closure
controls.lod_basis: First line of defense against slips closure during normal drilling operations

supporting_text: Once the downward force of the drill string overcame the force of the hydraulic pressure that was keeping the slips in the open position, the slips closed

controls.name: Rig repositioning to maintain proper drill string alignment
controls.barrier_role: Prevent drill string misalignment and excessive downward force on slips
controls.lod_basis: Operational control to maintain safe drilling geometry

supporting_text: Just prior to the incident, the Assistant Driller noticed that the drill string was rubbing against the rotary and slips. He called up to the Bridge of the ship to ask the crew if they could reposition the rig so as to straighten up the drill string passing through the rotary. The Bridge explained that they were unable to reposition due to the poor weather conditions at the time.

controls.name: I-BOP safety valve on top drive
controls.lod_basis: Safety system to isolate well in event of loss of control

supporting_text: The I-BOP is a safety valve, located on the top drive of the rig, which can be closed should the rig experience a well control event.
the bolts on two of the I-BOP's actuator plates to be sheared

controls.name: Bushing saver and guide installation
controls.barrier_role: Prevent drill string misalignment and slips closure
controls.lod_basis: Engineering modification to prevent future slips closure events

supporting_text: BHP and Transocean are looking into installing a bushing saver and guide that should prevent any future occurrences.

controls.name: I-BOP actuator plate and lever arm modification
controls.barrier_role: Prevent contact between actuator plates and lever arms during drilling
controls.lod_basis: Equipment design modification to prevent mechanical interference

supporting_text: This investigation discovered that the MHWirth/Aker I-BOP actuator plates and lever arms were making contact during drilling operations and that modifications to the equipment needed to be made to prevent a future occurrence.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
